title: Add read-side symlink-deny check — close /etc/shadow read via symlink

verification.test: 345/345 workspace vitest (+9: R1-R7 baseline + R8/R9 archive paths added post-review)

teamFlow.coder: arceus:coder — assertReadable + resolveAndAssertReadable + readText/createDownloadStream switch + R1-R7 tests
teamFlow.tester: arceus:tester — 5 gates green (343/343 at coder output)
teamFlow.reviewer: arceus:reviewer — REQUEST_CHANGES; 1 BLOCK (createArchiveStream+compressToDisk also read paths) + 2 WARN (stale class comment, TOCTOU note) + 4 SUGGEST; all BLOCK/WARN fixed + 3/4 SUGGESTs applied (the 4th — R7 cast cleanup — done by switching R7 to indirect readText test)

origin: check-spec multi-agent review (2026-05-27) — Layer 2 Files/Terminal review flagged that readText() and createDownloadStream() use fs.stat (follows symlinks) with no read-side deny-list analogous to DANGEROUS_WRITE_PATHS.
rootCause: The v0.1.1 security fix (075929b) hardened the WRITE path with assertWritable() over DANGEROUS_WRITE_PATHS, but symmetrical read-side protection was never added. FilesService.readText (apps/server/src/modules/files/files.service.ts:214) and createDownloadStream (files.service.ts:319) use fs.stat / fs.createReadStream which transparently follow symlinks. A user with write access to any path can create a symlink pointing to /etc/shadow, /root/.ssh/id_ed25519, or any other sensitive file, then read it through the panel's read APIs.
fixSummary: Introduce assertReadable() with a deny-list of read-sensitive paths (/etc/shadow, /etc/sudoers, /etc/ssh, /root, /home/*/.ssh, /var/lib/sqlite — i.e. tighter than the write deny-list because read leaks are subtler). Switch read-path stat() calls to lstat() + symlink resolution check, and reject if the realpath() target falls under the deny-list.
